title: Hard Pass
description: He made his move. She made her position very clear. Nature is not always romantic. Sometimes it is just awkward.
longDescription:
Barn swallows are spectacular little birds and this pair did not disappoint.

What you are looking at appears to be a male making his case, wings fully extended, arriving with all the confidence of someone who has not yet read the room. The female, perched and thoroughly unimpressed, made her position on the matter very clear.

The agility and speed of barn swallows makes them a genuine challenge to photograph. Getting a clean frame on one in motion is a small victory. Capturing a moment like this, the drama, the timing, the expression on her face, felt like winning something he definitely did not.

I photograph wildlife the way I believe it should be done. No bait, no calls, no disturbance. Just patience, a good lens, and occasionally being in exactly the right place at exactly the right moment.

No barn swallows were bothered in the making of this photograph. One may have had his feelings hurt, but that was between him and her.
tools: Tools: Sony a1 II & Sony FE 600mm f/4 GM OSS
